name: Corn Tortillas
recipeIngredient: 4 Cups finely ground deep yellow masa harina
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the masa harina, water, and salt. Stir until smooth. The dough should be slightly sticky and form a ball when pressed together.
2. To test, flatten a small ball of dough between your palms. If the edges crack, add water to the dough, one tablespoon at a time, until a test piece does not crack.
3. Divide the dough into 24 golf ball-size pieces for tacos or 12 large balls for quesadillas. Place on a platter and cover with a damp towel.
4. Line a tortilla press with two sheets of plastic cut from a plastic freezer bag or other heavy-duty plastic bag.
5. Heat a comal until moderately hot.
6. Flatten each ball of dough in the tortilla press. Remove the top sheet of plastic, holding the tortilla with your fingertips, and peel off the bottom sheet.
7. Lay the tortillas one by one on the comal and cook about 30 to 40 seconds per side, pressing the top of each tortilla with your fingertips to make it puff.
8. Place the hot tortillas in a towel. Serve immediately or let cool, wrap well in plastic or a plastic bag, and store in the refrigerator up to one week.
